calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Mar-18-2025 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-27-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
James Leestma, Successor Administrator in Pro Per.
Eric Giersch for and with Matt Freitas, Removed Administrator (via Zoom).

The court noted a discrepancy regarding the estate funds held at Pacific Bank vs. Banc of California. Mr. Leestma clarified that the banks merged, and funds remain FDIC insured through a sweep account mechanism that ensures full coverage.

The court inquired as to why a bank statement covering October 15 through November 11, 2024 was submitted when the accounting period ended October 11, 2024. Mr. Leestma explained that final funds from escrow were deposited into the estate account after the accounting period, and the provided statement was the first available to reflect that transaction.

The court addressed Mr. Leestma's extraordinary fee request and sought clarification. Mr. Leestma argued that these fees were justified under the Common Fund Doctrine and Substantial Benefit Doctrine. He stated that the work performed had led to the successful removal of the prior administrator, benefiting the estate. Matt Freitas, the sole heir, had consented to the final account, including these fees.
The court questioned whether the Common Fund Doctrine applied when no additional funds had been recovered. Mr. Leestma argued that case law supported awarding fees when legal efforts contributed to protecting and advancing the estate's interests.
The court inquired why the $1,150 had been referred to as "commissions" rather than extraordinary attorney fees. Mr. Leestma clarified that these fees had been earned for acting as successor administrator, not for legal services. The fees had been calculated on an hourly basis and documented in a verified declaration.

The court took the matter under submission for further review of the extraordinary fee request.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order To 1) Remove Administrator 2) Require Administrator To Sell Estate Real Property 3) Require Administrator To File A Final Account And Petition For Distribution 4) For Reimbursement Of Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Aug-28-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Huber appeared by zoom for and with Matthew Freitas (administrator) by zoom.
James Lestma appeared by zoom for Advanced Inheritance LLC (petitioner).

Mr. Huber stated that his client was making efforts to refinance or sell the property, but his efforts have not come to fruition at this point. Mr. Leestma stated that the property is already in foreclosure, that his client helped Mr. Freitas to clear foreclosure at that time, and that the property is not listed for sale and there are no efforts to refinance. Mr. Leestma also stated that Mr. Freitas is in breach of his fiduciary duties, that it is time for Mr. Freitas to be removed, that if he remains at the helm, the property will be lost in foreclosure and that no one is taking away his inheritance rights. Further discussion was held regarding the issue of whether there is a conflict of interest with Mr. Leestma being appointed. The report on status of administration is off calendar. The Petition for Order to Remove Administrator, etc., and the Petition for Letters of Administration are under submission with the court's intent to grant. The parties waive an accounting from Mr. Freitas.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Nguyen. Not reported.

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-28-2024 Continued To Aug-27-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Huber appeared by zoom for and with Matt Freitas by zoom.
James Leestma appeared by zoom for Advance Inheritance LLC (objector).

Mr. Huber stated that the inventory and appraisal was rejected because it had an incorrect address on it, that it was sent to Matt to sign, and that Matt should be getting it back to them. Mr. Leestma stated that this estate has a single asset with a single heir (Matt), that Matt received $85,000.00 from his client, that his client would like to see the estate being administered and that his client would like to see a plan for repayment either by sale of the property or refinance. Mr. Leestma also stated that his client does not want to wait a year and that he may be filing a petition for removal of the administrator. The court stated that a beneficiary themself cannot sell real property and that Mr. Leestma's client has the ability to file a lien, but it does not see how the court can force the sale of the real property given there is a contract with Matt as a beneficiary not an administrator. Mr. Huber suggested a 60 to 90-day continuance. The Report on Status of Administration is continued to August 27, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Roger T. Picquet. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.
